chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male presented with gastrointestinal bleeding.
history: Gastrointestinal metastases from primary lung adenocarcinoma are rare. The patient was asymptomatic until presenting in an advanced stage. A duodenal ulcer was found to be a metastatic lesion from primary lung adenocarcinoma.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: Imaging studies: duodenal ulcer identified as a metastatic lesion from primary lung adenocarcinoma. Immunochemistry used to differentiate between primary gastrointestinal cancer and lung cancer metastasis.
correct_diagnosis: Duodenal metastases from lung adenocarcinoma causing gastrointestinal bleeding
differentials: Duodenal metastases from lung adenocarcinoma causing gastrointestinal bleeding
correct_ranking: Duodenal metastases from lung adenocarcinoma causing gastrointestinal bleeding
